preamble: The shift from a nasalized open front vowel [ã] to the diphthong [ui] involves diphthongization. This shift typically occurs through a series of intermediate vowel qualities and loss of nasalization or its transformation into a glide.

examples.note: Dialectal variation in Portuguese often shows various nasal vowels breaking into rising or falling diphthongs like [ui].

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
